title: Mega Sableye Battle Guide
summary: A [magic-bounce] utility Mega that reflects [taunt], [stealth-rock], and [thunder-wave] while grinding opponents down with [will-o-wisp] and [recover].
strengths: [magic-bounce] reflects status and hazards for free
weaknesses: [Fairy] weakness is punished hard in current meta
85 Attack means offensive pressure is weak

recommendedMoves.reason: Core utility to burn physical attackers
[Dark] STAB that scales off the target's Attack

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternative

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Sableye is a utility pivot built entirely around [magic-bounce]. The ability reflects status moves and hazards — [taunt], [stealth-rock], [thunder-wave], [encore], [leech-seed], etc. — so it single-handedly disables most common support Pokemon. Paired with [will-o-wisp] and [recover], Mega Sableye forms one of the most resilient stallwalls in the format.

## Strengths
[magic-bounce] is arguably the best utility ability in the metagame. Stall teams cannot set [stealth-rock], support leads cannot land [taunt], and paralysis strategies fail outright. [Dark]/[Ghost] typing is immune to [Psychic] and [Fighting] — a very clean defensive pivot against common threats like [urshifu] and [mewtwo]. [recover] gives it long-term sustain that physical attackers cannot outpace.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Fairy] is the critical weakness in the current meta where [tapu-koko], [gardevoir], and friends are everywhere. 85 Attack means direct damage is weak — it leans hard on [foul-play] to punish setup sweepers instead. Speed 50 is very slow, so Mega Sableye only works as a switch-in, not a lead.

## Recommended Moveset
[will-o-wisp] is the primary answer to physical threats and pairs with [foul-play] to neuter attackers. [recover] is mandatory for the walling plan. [foul-play] uses the target's Attack stat, which means Mega Sableye actually becomes stronger against opposing setup sweepers. [shadow-ball] rounds out the set as a [Ghost] STAB.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[sablenite] is mandatory. [magic-bounce] activates only after Mega Evolution (the base form's [prankster] is not used). Tera [Fairy] flips the [Fighting], [Dark], and [Bug] weaknesses to resistances, while Tera [Dark] preserves the [Psychic] immunity and boosts [foul-play].

## Teammates & Synergy
Pair Mega Sableye with a strong special attacker to cover its weak offense. [Steel] or [Poison] teammates handle the [Fairy] weakness. It also fits naturally on [trick-room] cores where its low Speed becomes an asset.

## Play Patterns
Do not lead — pivot Mega Sableye in on a predicted status move or setup turn to bounce back a hazard or [taunt]. Burn incoming physical attackers, then use [recover] to outlast them. Use [foul-play] as a finisher on boosted sweepers.
